events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: primary/secondary landed reversed on transformers (dry-type & pad-mount). This means the primary and secondary conductors are landed on the wrong terminals - reversed. This is a serious defect that could cause overvoltage, equipment damage, safety hazards. At rough-complete stage.

Decision: fail - non-compliant, installed-defective.
Risk: critical - reversed primary/secondary could energize at wrong voltage, damage equipment, safety hazard.
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant.
Percent_complete: maybe 85 (rough-complete).
Confidence: high, 0.95.
Findings: primary and secondary conductors landed reversed on transformer terminals.
Actions: de-energize/lockout, re-land conductors per drawings and nameplate, verify phasing and voltage ratio before energization.
Rationale: Reversed primary/secondary landing contradicts manufacturer nameplate and engineered drawings; energizing would apply wrong voltage, risk equipment destruction and personnel hazard. NEC 450 requires transformers installed per nameplate; 250.30 grounding/bonding for separately derived systems must be correct.
References: NEC Art. 450, NEC 250.30.
